_comment: WC2026 Group G, MD3. Standings before: Egypt 4, Belgium 2 (1-1 Egypt, 0-0 Iran), Iran 2, NZ 1 (2-2 Iran, 1-3 Egypt). Belgium the clear quality side but BLUNT so far (1 goal in 2, frustrated by organized Egypt and Iran). NZ is OPEN, not a low block (2-2 Iran, conceded 3 to Egypt) -> the favourite+goals archetype (cf. Egypt-NZ, where backing favourite + goals was right). Both must-win-ish (Belgium for safety, NZ for any chance) -> open game. Belgium should finally break through vs an open NZ. Home = NZ, away = Belgium. Cards not modelled (referee TBD). RECALIBRATION: first pass sat 5.2 pts BELOW the market on Belgium (56.8% vs market-implied 62.0%). This is the recurring 'do not lean below an elite favourite vs an open opponent' lesson (Spain): NZ is open (not a low block) so there is no organized-underdog reason to fade Belgium. Lifted Belgium 1.85 -> 2.05 and trimmed NZ 1.00 -> 0.92 to align with the market.

meta.stakes: Belgium (2 pts) likely need a win to be safe; NZ (1 pt) must win for any chance. Belgium the clear favourite vs an open NZ; expect goals.

home.name: New Zealand
notes: Open side: drew Iran 2-2, lost 1-3 to Egypt (concedes and scores). attack 0.95.
Must win -> commits, opens the game further.

away.name: Belgium
notes: Quality but blunt vs organized sides (1 goal in 2); NZ is OPEN, not a low block -> Belgium breaks through. attack 1.40.
Favourite + goals archetype; aligned with market.

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (RotoWire/Sports Mole, ~60 min pre-KO). Two offsetting changes for Belgium: (a) Nathan Ngoy is SUSPENDED (red card vs Iran) -> Theate deputises, a small downgrade on the first-choice CB pairing; (b) Doku is back in the squad after paternity leave, restoring attacking depth. Otherwise a full-strength side (Courtois, De Bruyne, Tielemans, Trossard, Lukaku). New Zealand name an unchanged XI (Wood up top), still an open side. Net: the two Belgium changes roughly cancel on attack, but the missing first-choice CB nudges NZ's scoring up a touch (0.92 -> 0.98); Belgium's 2.05 holds. Still aligned with the market and HIGH confidence.

_postmatch.result: New Zealand 1-5 Belgium. Belgium routed an open NZ and topped Group G on goal difference (+4); New Zealand finish last (1 pt) and are out.
_postmatch.verdict: Full process hit. 1X2 favourite landed, and EVERY goals read was right: total 6 goals (Over 2.5 58.3% / Over 3.5 35.9% both in), BTTS Yes (54.9%). Most importantly the RECALIBRATION was vindicated: the first pass sat 5.2 pts below the market on Belgium and I lifted it on the 'do not fade an elite favourite vs an open side' lesson (Spain). Belgium had been blunt vs organized blocks (1 goal in 2 vs Egypt/Iran), but vs an OPEN New Zealand — not a low block — it finally broke through, exactly the archetype. The 5-1 margin is in the upside tail (Belgium lambda 2.05 -> 5 goals = finishing overperformance), but the direction, side, and goal volume were all correct.
_postmatch.lesson: Confirms two rules at once: (1) do not lean below an elite favourite when the opponent is OPEN rather than a parked bus — classify the underdog's setup first (cf. Egypt-NZ); (2) Belgium's earlier bluntness was opponent-shape-dependent, not a true downgrade. The margin overshoot is honest variance an elite attack produces against a stretched, must-win minnow.
